title: Question: Avoiding recursive make
body:
---


Author Name: **Scott Johnson**
Original Redmine Message: 1937 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Today I build my Verilog model by running verilator from a Makefile, then recursively running $(MAKE) to build the executable using the V*.mk that verilator generates.

Since recursive make is considered harmful[1], I'd like to include the generated Makefile directly from mine instead of recursing. But the generated Vtoy.mk is in obj_dir and assumes that obj_dir is the current path, resulting in the rules not matching the expected files any more.

```
$ make -I obj_dir
make: *** No rule to make target 'obj_dir/Vtoy__ALL.a', needed by 'obj_dir/Vtoy'.  Stop.

```

Is there any supported way for me to use make include on Vtoy.mk instead of having to recursively run `$(MAKE) -f Vtoy.mk`?


[1] http://aegis.sourceforge.net/auug97.pdf
code_blocks:
$ make -I obj_dir
make: *** No rule to make target 'obj_dir/Vtoy__ALL.a', needed by 'obj_dir/Vtoy'.  Stop.
